body.p:
Executive Mansion,
Washington,
1863
Four score and seven years ago our fathers brought forth, upon this continent, a new nation, concerueda in liberty, and dedicated to the proposition that"all men are created equal"

Now we are engaged in a great civil war, testing wheather that nation,
or any nation so concieird, and so dedicated, can long endure. Me are met on a great battles fields of that war. We have come to dedicate a portion of it, aa a fined rest-
ing places for those who once here,
that the nation might live. This we do, in all propriety, too. Butt,
in a larger sense, we can not dedicate_ we caan not consecrat_ we
can not hallow, this grouads_
_the brave men, living and dead, who strugglesh
her, have hallowed it, far above our poor poowers
to add or detract. The worlnds nics littel note,
norlong remember what we say hear, whils it can never forget what
they did here.
It is rather for us, the living, to stand here,
